factors: Mostly I was looking at whether the farmer changed his course of action after the wizard acted.  Then, to me, it seemed like the wizard caused the farmer to go for a basket other than the one he had initially set out for.  I thought this because it seemed like the wizard added extra fruit to the basket which was an added incentive for the farmer to change course.  Otherwise I chose that the wizard did not have any impact on the farmer's action.  I don't think any of the things I saw seemed like the wizard was allowing or enabling the farmer to take a course of action, because the farmer's path to either basket wasn't blocked or obstructed, and the farmer's motivations weren't clear.  I didn't believe that the farmer had a secret desire to go to the left basket, but it didn't have as much fruit, so he headed to the right basket, but then the wizard enabled/allowed the farmer to go to the left basket by adding fruit to it.  That didn't make sense to me as an interpretation of what was happening.
